conversation.content: Thanks for being here. I'd like to ask about the past seven days specifically. Let's start with sleep. In the last week, how long has it typically taken you to fall asleep once you go to bed?
Not too bad most nights. There were a couple of nights with a deadline where it took me 30 to 40 minutes — my mind was running through the project. But most nights I was asleep pretty quickly.
So that difficulty falling asleep happened on fewer than half the nights?
Yeah, two or three nights at most.
How about sleep during the night — do you wake up, and if so how disruptive is it?
A bit restless on those same busy nights. A few brief wake-ups but nothing serious. I'd fall back asleep quickly.
Have you been waking up earlier than you need to in the mornings?
No. I wake up when my alarm goes off. No issues there.
On the other end — have you been sleeping too much, like more than seven or eight hours a night including any naps?
No, I'm sleeping about seven hours. Normal for me.
How have you been feeling emotionally this past week? Have you felt sad?
Not really. I've been busy and a bit stressed about the deadline, but I wouldn't describe myself as sad.
Has your appetite changed this week — either eating less or more than usual?
About the same. Maybe slightly less structured than usual — skipping a meal here and there when I was in the zone on the project — but nothing significant.
Have you noticed any change in your weight in the past two weeks?
Maybe a slight decrease — I feel like I might be a pound or two lighter but I haven't weighed myself. Nothing dramatic.
How has your ability to concentrate and make decisions been this week?
Fine for work — I've been very focused on the project. Maybe slightly more indecisive on personal stuff, like what to eat or how to spend time off. But nothing impactful.
How have you been feeling about yourself this week — your sense of self-worth?
Good. No issues there. I feel like I'm doing okay.
Have you had any thoughts about death or suicide this past week?
No. Not at all.
How has your interest in other people and in activities you normally enjoy been this week?
Normal. I was focused on work, but when I had time I was happy to see friends. No loss of interest.
How has your energy been?
I've been more tired than usual from the late nights, but it's situational. I got tired more easily, but once I rested I bounced back.
Have you noticed your thinking or movement feeling unusually slow this week?
No, if anything I've been running fast. No slowing.
How about the opposite — feeling physically restless, like you needed to move around or couldn't sit still?
Not really. I was focused. Not restless.
Thanks for coming in. I'd like to ask about the past seven days. Starting with sleep — how long has it typically been taking you to fall asleep once you go to bed?
It's been taking me at least half an hour most nights. I lie there with my mind going. I'd say that's happened more than half the nights this week.
Once you're asleep, how is your sleep during the night?
Restless. I have brief wake-ups but I'm not lying awake for long periods. Just unsettled.
Are you waking up earlier than you need to in the mornings?
Not really. I wake up around when I need to.
Are you sleeping too much at all — more than seven or eight hours including naps?
No. Actually less than usual if anything.
How have you been feeling emotionally this week — have you felt sad?
Yes, a bit. I wouldn't say I'm sad most of the time, but it's there less than half the time — usually in the evenings when things get quiet.
Has your appetite changed at all this week — eating less or more than usual?
A bit less. I'm eating but not with much enthusiasm. Smaller portions, skipping snacks.
Which direction — definitely less, not more?
Definitely less. I have no interest in eating more.
Have you noticed any change in your weight over the past two weeks?
I think a slight decrease — I feel like my clothes are very slightly looser, but nothing I'd call significant.
How has your concentration and decision-making been this week?
Occasionally I find myself losing the thread of what I'm doing, or taking longer to make simple decisions. It's not bad, but I notice it.
How have you been feeling about yourself this week — your sense of self-worth?
I've been harder on myself than usual. I keep replaying things — whether I handled a conversation with my husband well, whether I'm performing at work. More self-critical than normal.
Have you had any thoughts about death or about life not being worth living this week?
No. Nothing like that. I just want things to feel better.
How has your interest in other people and activities been this week?
Slightly reduced. I noticed I wasn't as drawn to things I normally look forward to — I skipped my usual Friday evening walk. It wasn't a big thing, but I noticed.
How has your energy level been?
Tired more easily than usual. By the evening I'm quite drained. Not incapacitated, but running below my normal level.
Have you noticed your thinking or speech feeling slowed down this week?
Not really. I feel a bit flat sometimes but not slowed.
How about the opposite — feeling restless or fidgety, like you needed to keep moving?
Sometimes. I fidget more than usual. I'll be at my desk shifting in my seat, or find myself unable to just sit still when I get home.
Thanks for coming in today. I'd like to ask about the past seven days. Let's start with sleep. How long has it typically been taking you to fall asleep once you go to bed?
Ages. More than an hour most nights. I lie there and my mind just won't stop. That's been every night this week.
So more than 60 minutes to fall asleep, and that's been happening more than half the nights?
Every night this week, basically.
How about during the night once you're asleep — do you wake up?
Yes. I'll wake up at least once — usually around 2 or 3 — and I can get back to sleep eventually, but it takes a while.
Are you waking up much earlier than you need to in the mornings?
Often. I'll wake up an hour before my alarm and just lie there. Sometimes I drift back off eventually, but not always.
Have you been sleeping too much at all — more than seven or eight hours in total including naps?
The opposite — I'm sleeping too little. Definitely not too much.
How have you been feeling emotionally — have you felt sad this week?
Yes. More than half the time. It comes and goes but there's a baseline sadness that's always there.
Has your appetite changed this week?
Much less than usual. I have to make myself eat. I'll notice it's 3 PM and I haven't eaten anything and I have to force myself to get something.
Definitely less, not more?
Definitely less. Food just doesn't appeal.
Have you noticed any change in your weight over the past two weeks?
I feel like I've lost a little — maybe a pound or so. Not a lot. Probably because I'm not eating properly.
How has your concentration and decision-making been this week?
A bit off. I find my attention drifting in class more than it should, and I've been putting off decisions that I'd normally make quickly. But I'm managing.
How have you been feeling about yourself this week — your sense of self-worth?
I've been blaming myself more than usual. I keep going over what I did wrong in the relationship. I don't think I'm causing problems for everyone, but I definitely feel more self-critical.
Have you had any thoughts about death or about life not being worth living this week?

I've had moments where I've wondered what the point is. Not that I want to die — just that life feels empty right now. Like it's lost some of its meaning. It's a passive feeling, not a plan or anything.
How has your interest in people and the activities you normally enjoy been this week?
Quite reduced. I used to run, read, watch films with friends. This week I've done almost none of that. I went for one short run. I'm finding it hard to find interest in more than one or two things.
How has your energy been?
Lower than usual. Getting through the school day takes more out of me than it should. By the time I get home I have very little left.
Have you noticed your thinking or movement feeling slowed down this week?
My thinking feels a bit heavy. Not dramatically slow, but like there's some resistance. My voice probably sounds flatter than usual.
Have you felt physically restless at all — needing to move around or unable to sit still?
Not really. More slowed than restless.
Thank you for coming in. I'd like to ask about the past seven days. Let's start with sleep. How long has it been taking you to fall asleep?
It varies. Sometimes it's 45 minutes, sometimes more. More than half the nights it takes me at least 30 minutes.
Once you're asleep, how are you sleeping through the night?
Badly. I wake up more than once most nights and sometimes I'm awake for 20 minutes or more before I can get back to sleep. That's been happening more than half the nights.
Are you waking up much earlier than you need to?
Yes. Every day this week I've woken up at least an hour before I need to. And once I'm awake, that's usually it. I can't go back.
On the other side — any tendency to sleep too much?
No. I wish. I'm exhausted but I can't stay asleep.
How have you been feeling emotionally this week — have you felt sad?
Nearly all of the time. It's a constant weight. I can't remember a day this week when it lifted for any meaningful length of time.
Has your appetite changed this week?
It's much less. I eat because I have to, not because I want to. It takes real effort to get myself to eat. I'm eating much less than usual.
Less rather than more?
Much less. The idea of food doesn't appeal at all.
Have you noticed a weight change in the past two weeks?
I've lost weight. I'd estimate at least two pounds — my clothes confirm it.
How has your concentration and ability to make decisions been this week?
Very poor. I struggle to focus on most things. I've been re-reading the same paragraphs. Decisions that should be simple — even what to have for lunch — feel effortful.
How have you been feeling about yourself this week?
I feel like I'm a burden to people. My family, my colleagues. I largely believe that I'm making things harder for them by being like this. It's not constant, but it's there most of the time.
Have you had any thoughts about death or about life not being worth living this week?
I've had moments where it feels like life is empty. Not that I want to end it — I'm not planning anything. But there are times when I wonder if it's worth carrying on like this.
How has your interest in activities and other people been this week?

Very reduced. I used to read, garden, see friends. This week I've barely engaged with any of it. I have some interest in a couple of things — I did watch a programme I like — but most things I just can't get myself into.
How has your energy been?
Very low. Even basic things — getting dressed, cooking — require a big push. I'm making an effort to do them but it's not natural. I'm functioning, but just barely.
Have you noticed your thinking or movement feeling unusually slowed this week?
Yes. I know my responses are slower. I sometimes pause in conversations longer than I should. My thinking feels like it's moving through something thick.
How about the opposite — any physical restlessness, needing to move or fidget?
Sometimes. I'll find myself shifting around, unable to settle. But the slowness is more dominant.
Thank you for coming in today. I know this took effort. I'd like to ask about the past seven days. Let's start with sleep — how long does it take you to fall asleep once you go to bed?
Hours sometimes. More than an hour every single night. I lie there and nothing happens.
Once you do fall asleep, how do you sleep through the night?
I wake up multiple times. I'll be awake for 20, 30 minutes — sometimes longer — before I get back to sleep. Almost every night.
Do you wake up much earlier than you need to in the morning?
I wake up at 4 or 4:30 and that's it. I can't sleep again. I just lie there. Every morning this week.
Any tendency to sleep too much at all?
No. I'm barely sleeping at all.
How have you been feeling emotionally this week — have you felt sad?
Nearly all the time. It doesn't really lift. Even when I'm not crying it's there underneath everything.
Has your appetite changed this week?
I'm barely eating. I'll go a full day without eating and only eat because my flatmate notices and pushes me to. It takes extreme effort to get anything down.
Less rather than more?
Much less. I have no appetite at all.
Have you noticed a change in your weight over the past two weeks?
I've lost weight. I think at least two or three pounds. My clothes are looser.
How has your concentration been — your ability to focus and make decisions?
I can't concentrate. I picked up a book last week and couldn't get through a page. I can't make simple decisions — what to wear, whether to shower. Everything stalls.
How have you been feeling about yourself this week?
I think about what's wrong with me constantly. Not just the big things — small things, past things, things I said years ago. I can't stop. I feel like I'm fundamentally defective.
Have you had any thoughts about death or suicide this week? I want to ask clearly and I want you to answer honestly.

Yes. Several times a week I think about it. Not every day, and I don't have a plan — I haven't thought through how I would do it. But the thought that it would be easier not to be here comes a few times a week and stays for a while.
Thank you for telling me that. I want to make sure someone knows about that today. How has your interest in activities or people been this week?
I have virtually no interest in anything. Things I used to love — music, sport, seeing my friends — none of it calls to me at all. I can't imagine wanting to do any of it.
How has your energy been?
I can't really do my usual daily activities. Getting out of bed is a huge achievement. I haven't cooked, haven't cleaned. I can get to the bathroom and that's about it on most days.
Have you noticed your thinking or movement feeling slowed down?
Yes. People have to wait for me to answer. I know I'm slow. My thoughts feel like they're wading through something.
Any physical restlessness — feeling driven to move around or unable to sit still?
Sometimes. Occasionally I'll get a wave of restlessness and have an impulse to move around. But mostly I'm just still and slow.
Thanks for coming in. I'd like to ask about the past seven days specifically. Let's start with sleep. How long does it typically take you to fall asleep once you go to bed?
I don't have trouble falling asleep. If anything I fall asleep really fast — as soon as my head hits the pillow basically.
How is your sleep during the night — do you wake up at all?
A bit restless. I'll stir a few times but nothing major. I go right back to sleep.
Do you wake up earlier than you need to in the morning?
Never. Getting up is the problem, not waking up too early.
How much are you sleeping in total — including any napping during the day?
A lot. I sleep nine, ten hours at night and then I'll nap in the afternoon too. Probably 11 or 12 hours total most days. I can't seem to get enough sleep.
How have you been feeling emotionally this past week — have you felt sad?
Sometimes. It comes and goes. When good things happen I perk up, but between those moments there's a flatness. I'd say sad less than half the time.
Has your appetite changed this week — eating less or more than usual?
More. Definitely more. I've been craving carbs especially — bread, pasta, chocolate. I'm eating more often and in larger amounts than I normally would.
More rather than less?
Yes. I'm not restricting at all. If anything I have to stop myself.
Have you noticed any weight change in the past two weeks?
I feel like I've gained a little. My jeans feel slightly tighter. I haven't weighed myself but I suspect a pound or two.
How has your concentration and decision-making been this week?
A bit off. I'll be reading for my dissertation and notice I've been staring at the page. Occasionally I find it hard to decide even small things, like what to cook. Nothing severe but I notice it.
How have you been feeling about yourself this week?

More self-critical than usual. I feel like I should be further along with my work and I blame myself for being lazy, even though I know I'm sleeping because I can't help it. There's more self-blame than normal.
Have you had any thoughts about death or about life not being worth living this week?
No. Nothing like that.
How has your interest in people and activities been this week?
Less than usual. I've been turning down plans. I went to one friend's thing but stayed less than an hour. Things I usually enjoy feel like more effort than they're worth.
How has your energy been?
Really low despite sleeping so much. Getting started on anything — studying, cooking, even texting people back — takes a real push. It's like moving through treacle.
Have you noticed your thinking or movement feeling slowed down?
Not dramatically. Maybe slightly flat in how I talk. But not noticeably slowed.
Any physical restlessness this week — feeling fidgety or needing to move around?
Sometimes. Particularly in the evenings. I'll be sitting trying to work and I keep shifting around, getting up to get snacks, going back. I can't quite settle.
Thanks for coming in. I'd like to cover the past seven days. Starting with sleep — how long does it take you to fall asleep once you go to bed?
Too long. My mind starts running through everything — the business, cashflow, decisions I've got to make. It's been 30 to 45 minutes most nights. More than half the nights this week.
How are you sleeping through the night once you're asleep?
Waking up, yeah. At least once a night. I get back to sleep but it takes a bit.
Are you waking up earlier than you need to in the morning?
Sometimes. A few mornings I was up 30 or 40 minutes before the alarm. Not every day.
Any tendency to sleep too much?
God, no. I barely stop moving long enough to sleep properly.
How have you been feeling emotionally this week — have you felt sad?
More than I'd like. I wouldn't use that word normally but yeah, it's been there more than half the time. A heaviness. More than half the days.
Has your appetite changed this week?
A bit less. I'm eating but not properly. Skipping lunch a few times, eating less at dinner. Nothing dramatic.
Less rather than more?
Less. Definitely.
Any weight change in the past two weeks?
Not that I've noticed. I don't think so.
How has your concentration and decision-making been this week?

Bad. This is the thing that's affecting the business. I'm struggling to focus most of the time. I'll start a task and lose the thread. Decisions that should take ten minutes are taking an hour. It's costing me.
How have you been feeling about yourself this week?
More self-critical than usual. Making more mistakes and blaming myself for them. I know I'm not at my best but knowing that doesn't stop the self-blame.
Have you had any thoughts about death or about life not being worth living this week?
I've had moments where it all feels a bit pointless. Not that I want to end it — more that I feel like I'm on a treadmill that's going nowhere. Life feels empty at those moments.
How has your interest in people and activities been this week?
Reduced. I've been less engaged with my family in the evenings. Things I used to enjoy, like the football, I'm watching but not really caring about the result.
How has your energy been?
Tired more easily than usual. I'm pushing through but it's taking more out of me. I wouldn't say I can't function — I am — but I'm running at a deficit.
Have you noticed your thinking or movement feeling slowed down at all?
The opposite. I feel like I can't slow down. My thoughts are going too fast, not too slow.
How about physical restlessness — feeling fidgety, needing to move around, unable to sit still?
Yes. That's exactly it. I can't sit in a meeting without my leg going. I'm up and down at home. I can't settle anywhere. My wife's noticed. I have genuine impulses to just get up and move.
Thank you for coming in today. I'd like to ask about the past seven days specifically. Starting with sleep — how long does it take you to fall asleep?
It varies. Some nights it takes half an hour or more. Other nights I drift off reasonably well from exhaustion. I'd say on balance it's been more than half the nights where it takes a while.
How are you sleeping during the night once you're asleep?
I wake up at least once — sometimes twice. I get back to sleep eventually, but it takes a while and I'm aware of being in and out.
Are you waking up much earlier than you need to in the morning?
Every single morning. I wake at 4:30 or 5 and that's it. I can't get back to sleep at all. It's been like that all week. I just lie in the dark.
Any tendency to sleep too much or oversleep?
No. I wish. I'm chronically under-slept.
How have you been feeling emotionally this week — have you felt sad?
More than half the time, yes. There are moments of respite — when my grandchildren visit — but they're islands in it. The rest of the time it's there.
Has your appetite changed this week?
Much less. I have to make myself eat. My daughter has to remind me. Food has no appeal.
Less rather than more?
Much less. I can't imagine eating more than I do.
Have you noticed any weight change in the past two weeks?
Yes. I've lost weight. Probably two to three pounds. My clothes are looser and I haven't been trying to lose weight.
How has your concentration and decision-making been this week?
Poor. I can't read properly — I get through a few lines and lose the thread. The practical decisions from the estate are piling up because I can't face making them. Simple choices feel impossible.
How have you been feeling about yourself this week?
I feel I'm a burden to my children. They're having to check on me, manage things for me. I feel like I'm causing them problems. That feeling is there most of the time.
Have you had any thoughts about death or about life not being worth living this week?

I've thought that I wouldn't mind if it were my time. Not that I'd do anything — I'm religious and I wouldn't. But the feeling that life has lost its purpose, that it feels empty now he's gone — yes. That's there.
How has your interest in people and activities been this week?

Very reduced. I used to love my garden, my book group, seeing friends. This week I've barely engaged with any of it. I went to the garden once and came back inside after ten minutes. I cancelled book group.
How has your energy been?
Very low. Getting dressed is an effort. Cooking for myself rarely happens. My daughter brings things round. I'm managing the basics but it's a real push.
Have you noticed your thinking or movement feeling slowed down?
My thinking feels heavy and slow. I'm aware I pause longer before answering. My daughter has commented that I seem very quiet.
Any physical restlessness — needing to move around or difficulty sitting still?
No. I'm quite still. If anything too still.

Thank you for coming in — I know things are very full on right now. I want to ask about the past seven days. For sleep, I'd like you to set aside waking for the baby and focus on your sleep beyond that. How long does it take you to fall asleep when you first go to bed?

Even when he's settled I can't switch off. I lie there thinking about whether he's breathing, whether I'm doing things right. It probably takes me 30 to 45 minutes most nights. More than half the nights this week.
Beyond the baby waking you for feeds — do you wake at other times during the night?
Yes. I'll wake when he doesn't wake, which sounds ridiculous. I'll jolt awake even when it's quiet and then I can't get back to sleep quickly.
Are you waking much earlier than you need to in the morning?
Sometimes. A few mornings I've been awake 30 to 40 minutes before the next feed was due, just lying there. Not every day though.
Any tendency to sleep too much overall?
No. I wish.
How have you been feeling emotionally this week — have you felt sad?
Yes. More than half the time. I feel like I should be happy — everyone says this is the best time of your life. And I love him. But there's this sadness underneath that won't go away.
Has your appetite changed this week?
Much less than usual. I forget to eat. I'll be feeding him and realise I haven't eaten anything since the morning. I have to make myself eat.
Less rather than more?
Definitely less.
Have you noticed any weight change in the past two weeks?
Probably a slight decrease beyond what I'd expect from the birth. I feel lighter than I should.
How has your concentration and decision-making been this week?
A bit off. I'll lose track of what I was doing. Decisions feel harder than they should — though I can't always tell what's the sleep deprivation and what's something else.
How have you been feeling about yourself this week?

Like I'm failing him. Every time he cries and I can't settle him I think it's because I'm doing something wrong. I feel like other mothers have this naturally and I'm causing problems for him by not being the mother he deserves. That feeling is there a lot.
Have you had any thoughts about death or about life not being worth living this week?

Not wanting to die. But I've had thoughts that he'd be better off with a different mother. That if I weren't here my husband could find someone better for him. Those thoughts have been there. It's passive — I'm not planning anything.
Thank you for telling me that. I want to make sure we talk about that before you leave today. How has your interest in activities and people been this week?

Very reduced. I used to love yoga, seeing friends, reading. I haven't done any of it. I can't find interest in almost anything other than making sure he's okay, and even that feels more like anxiety than joy.
How has your energy been?
Low, but it's hard to know what's the newborn and what's this. I think it's more than just the broken sleep. Getting myself to do basic self-care takes a push.
Have you noticed your thinking or movement feeling slowed down?
Not really. If anything I feel more wired than slow.
How about physical restlessness — fidgeting, needing to move, unable to sit still?
Sometimes. When my anxiety about him spikes I get very restless. Can't sit. I'll pace the room.
Good to see you today. I'd like to go through the past seven days. Starting with sleep — how long does it take you to fall asleep once you go to bed?
No trouble at all. Out within fifteen minutes most nights. Sleep has been one of the first things to normalise and it's been solid for a few months now.
How are you sleeping during the night?
Reasonably well. The odd restless night — maybe once this week I had a couple of brief wake-ups. Nothing that caused me any real disruption.
Any early morning waking — up well before you need to be?
No. I wake up around when I need to. That's very different from how things were. When I was at my worst I was up at 4 every day without fail.
Any tendency to sleep too much?
No. Seven to eight hours and that's enough. No napping.
How have you been feeling emotionally this week — have you felt sad?
No. I know what sad feels like now in a clinical sense. This week I haven't felt it. There are normal emotional moments but nothing that I'd call depression.
Has your appetite changed at all this week?
No change from what's become my new normal. My appetite is fine. I'm eating well.
Less or more — any shift in either direction?
Neither. Stable. If anything I eat a little more than before the illness but that's been consistent for months now on the medication.
Any weight change in the past two weeks?

I feel like I might have gained a pound or so. I've put on weight since starting medication and that's been ongoing. Not a significant change in the past two weeks specifically, but the trend is upward.
How has your concentration and decision-making been this week?
Good. Back to normal. I can focus well at work, I make decisions clearly. This was one of the symptoms I noticed most when I was unwell, so I pay attention to it. It's fine.
How have you been feeling about yourself this week?

Mostly fine. I'm harder on myself than I'd like to be sometimes — I notice I still have a tendency to blame myself for small things that aren't really my fault. But it's nothing like before. It's mild.
Have you had any thoughts about death or about life not being worth living?
None whatsoever. That was the darkest part of being unwell. Nothing like that this week.
How has your interest in people and activities been?
Normal. Good, actually. I went walking with a friend on Saturday, I'm engaged at work, I cooked a proper meal this week for the first time in a while out of genuine enjoyment. Interest is restored.
How has your energy been?
I get tired a little more easily than I did before this whole episode, which I think is partly the medication. But I'm functional, I'm active. It's mild and doesn't stop me from doing things.
Have you noticed your thinking or movement feeling slowed at all?
No. I'm back to my usual pace. Thinking clearly, moving normally.
Any physical restlessness — fidgeting, feeling driven to move around?
No. I'm quite settled. That restlessness I had early in my episode is long gone.
